name: Ratio of pressure change (RPC)
variables: PA: acclimation pressure [kPa]; PN: nadir (exposure) pressure [kPa]

name: RPC (exposure/acclimation convention)
variables: PN: exposure/nadir pressure [kPa]; PA: acclimation pressure [kPa]
notes: INVERSE of Pflugrath/Brown RPC; never pool across the two conventions

name: Boyle's-law barotrauma (volume change)
variables: PA: acclimation pressure [kPa]; VA: gas volume at acclimation; PN: nadir pressure [kPa]; VN: gas volume at nadir
domain_of_validity: rapid decompression; physoclist & physostome swim bladder
notes: Brown 2012 contrasts Boyle's vs Henry's-law/dynamic effects

name: Log ratio of pressure (LRP)
variables: PA: acclimation pressure [kPa]; PN: nadir pressure [kPa]
notes: linearises the dose for logistic injury models

name: Logistic (mortal) injury vs ln(RPC)
variables: p: probability of injury/mortal injury; RPC: PA/PN
parameters: b0, b1 per species (see source Fig 2-3 / Table)
domain_of_validity: tested ln(RPC) range; bass, carp gudgeon, Murray cod, silver perch
validation: fit to trial data with 95% CI

name: Mortal-injury metric (AIC-selected)
equation: fish = mortally injured if any of 8 injury types present; model selected by AIC = 2k - 2 ln(L)
variables: k: number of parameters; L: likelihood; 8 binary injury indicators
parameters: AIC selects the predictive injury set
notes: defines the response variable reused across the PNNL/WA barotrauma studies

name: Mortal-injury logistic (LRP + tag burden)
variables: p: P(mortal injury); LRP: ln(PA/PN); TB: tag burden [%]
domain_of_validity: juvenile Chinook; tested LRP and tag-burden range
validation: worked example RPC=2.18 -> 24.3%/13.7% by tag burden
notes: exact functional form (logit vs 1-exp) to verify from source

name: Per-species mortal-injury logistic
variables: p: P(mortal injury); dose: pressure-ratio metric
domain_of_validity: European potamodromous incl. Cypriniformes; by swim-bladder morphology

name: LS10 lethal-shear threshold
equation: LS10 = strain rate causing +10% mortality above handling control
notes: shear metric defined in a barotrauma-corpus guideline

name: CFD-coupled barotrauma performance (BioPA-style)
equation: P(mortal injury) = integral of barotrauma dose-response over CFD nadir-pressure distribution
notes: couples CFD exposure to biological dose-response (Richmond/Pflugrath lineage)

name: HBET hydropower biological evaluation toolset
equation: HBET maps measured/modelled nadir and LRP to mortal-injury probability via dose-response

name: Blade-strike survival (multiple linear regression)
equation: P(S) = 1 + m*(V - Vcrit), bounded [0,1]; P(M) = 1 - P(S)
variables: V: strike velocity [m/s]; L/t: fish length / blade leading-edge thickness [-]
parameters: m: slope (tabulated per L/t ratio); Vcrit: critical velocity at which survival starts to drop
notes: m and Vcrit are tabulated for each L/t ratio (Table 3)

name: Blade-strike mortality (logistic in velocity & L/t)
parameters: b0,b1,b2 species-specific (b2 omitted when L/t not used)
notes: models without an L/t term drop b2 (e.g. bluegill); hybrid striped bass uses L/t

name: Blade-strike whole-fish mortality (curvilinear)
equation: P(M): log-logistic f/(1+(V/e)^b) for V<=x, then linear continuation (slope m) for V>x
parameters: b: steepness; e: ED50; f: max P(M); m: linear slope; x: logistic->linear transition
notes: BioPA/HBET-integrated; explorer plots the dominant log-logistic component, the high-velocity linear tail (m,x) is omitted

name: Fluid-shear injury/mortality (logistic in strain rate)
parameters: b0,b1 species- and endpoint-specific (minor/major injury, mortality)
response_metric: probability of injury / major injury / mortality
notes: one coefficient set per species x endpoint; LD50 strain rate = -b0/b1

name: Strain rate (fluid-shear dose)
variables: U: mean water velocity [cm/s]; y: distance perpendicular to flow [cm]
parameters: dy fixed at 1.8 cm (fish-width convention, Neitzel 2000)
notes: the chosen dy strongly affects magnitude; all PNNL models use dy=1.8 cm so values are comparable
